title: The Ridge Where Singing Becomes Silence

features.name: the wind-bell that forgot to ring
features.desc-on-touch: you touch where the bell once hung; the wind still moves, but the clapper is gone—or maybe it learned to move without sound

features.name: the stone marker
features.desc-on-touch: a carved mark in stone; the ridge marks its own boundary with something older than language

features.name: the shepherd's refuge
features.desc-on-touch: a small stone shelter where someone sheltered long ago; the walls hold the shape of silence waiting

features.name: the step-worn path
features.desc-on-touch: the ridge remembers where feet stopped listening; each step marked a moment when the singing lost its listener

features.name: the listening stone
features.desc-on-touch: a depression in the ridge where water gathers after rain; it was hollowed by listening, and now it reflects only sky

inscription: the singing stopped; the ridge now teaches silence to anyone who still listens

file.name: Singing_Path_Terminus_Recordings.mp3
file.note: field recordings of the ridge at sunset, where the path's song dissolves into wind-sound and eventual stillness

exits.label: back up toward where the singing still echoes
downward to where silence becomes its own kind of ground
eastward where the wind has learned to speak for the voices
